title: Triumph in Achievement
html:
Lucas stands there for a moment longer, then crouches and wraps both hands around the spear shaft. He works it out of the pile slowly, twisting as he pulls, until it comes free. He opens one of the water bottles from the bag and pours it over his forearm and down his leg where the blood has dried in lines, then wipes it off on the back of his outer shirt, which he already dropped in the meat section. He's running out of spare shirts.

The cleaver is still in the neighbor zombie's skull, blade deep, handle sticking up at an angle. He looks at it for a second. He doesn't want to carry it. Using a cleaver that close, on someone he recognized, in that specific way — there's a category of things he'd rather keep at arm's length from now on, and this is the first item in it. He sets his foot on the handle and presses down until the blade seats further in, and leaves it there.

He picks up the bag and the spear and is already turning for the back exit when the system sounds in his ear.

---

[Level 2 Zombie Killed, Awarding 100 Exp!]

---

Lucas stops. He turns back and looks at the pile. The neighbor zombie, the one that came in last and landed on top of the other two, the one he almost didn't kill. Level 2. He looks at the cleaver handle sitting in the skull. 'That explains the arm,' he thinks. A Level 2 zombie swinging a half-broken arm like a timber makes considerably more sense than a Level 1 doing it.

"The easiest Level 2 I've met so far," he mutters, which is technically true and also about as much credit as he's willing to give it. He turns away again.

A second notification follows before he reaches the exit.

---

Achievement Clear:

[Gold] Kill One Level 2 and Nine Level 1 Zombies in Two Days.

Rewards:

Inventory Pouch (0/100) [Epic]

Basic Dagger [Common]

---

Two items materialize on the fish stall counter beside him: a small golden-colored pouch about the size of his palm, and a plain black dagger roughly as long as the cleaver he just left in the floor. He stares at them. "Did I meet some clearing condition?" he says. He picks both of them up.

---

[System Items Detected, Updating System Profile!]

---

He turns the pouch over in his hand. It's flat and light and doesn't rattle when he shakes it. He reaches into his bag, pulls out the shampoo bottle, and drops it into the pouch. The bottle disappears. The pouch doesn't change shape. He reaches back into the pouch and pulls the shampoo bottle out again and stands holding it with an expression that takes a second to resolve. 'I feel like I just did something illegal.'

He puts the shampoo back in. He thinks, briefly, about white powder and border crossings and how useful this would have been before the world ended. It would have been perfect. He shakes his head and gets to work.

It takes a few minutes to transfer everything from the backpack into the pouch: the canned tins, the water bottles, the orange juice and soy milk, the soaps and shampoo, the sunflower seed bags, the snack packs, the phone and power bank and charger, the notebook and pen, the bandage tape. Everything except the bat. The pouch stays flat and palm-sized through all of it. He folds the empty backpack and tucks it inside the pouch too, then slides the pouch into his pants pocket, where it sits without bulk. He does not think about this for too long because thinking about it too long makes it harder to accept.

He picks up the black dagger. It's sharp along the edge and balanced like something that was made to be used rather than displayed, which is more than he expected from something that appeared out of thin air. It doesn't look as useful as the spear for most situations, but it's a system item, which probably means something, and it's small enough to carry without thinking about it. He puts it in his pocket next to the pouch.

"Opening System," he says.

---

Name: Lucas Tharla

Age: 16 years old

Race: Human

Gender: Male

Class: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Level: 4 (260/300)

Stats:

Agi: 14 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Str: 24 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Int: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Vit: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Unallocated Stats Point: 0

Skills:

Unallocated Skills Point: 0

Coins:

Achievements Completed: 1 ᐃ

Items: 2 ᐃ

---

40 EXP from Level 5. Coins as a new field, empty. He hasn't earned any yet, or hasn't earned any the system is counting. He stares at it. "I haven't even figured out how to get skills, and now there are coins," he mutters. The system offers nothing on either point. He closes it and walks out the back exit of the market.

The sunlight outside hits him in the face and he stops. "Whoa." He squints. He's been inside for a while and the interior of the market was dim even before the power went out, and the contrast between that and direct morning sun is enough to make him stand at the exit for a few seconds with his eyes adjusting.

The road behind the market is empty and wide, the same as the road in front. He starts walking.

He has known most of the people in this village by sight his whole life — the vendor families at the market, the neighbors along the lake path, the construction crew he worked with last dry season. Of the ten zombies he's killed over the past two days, several were probably people he'd seen before. He didn't stop to check every face. He finds, walking in the sunlight, that he's glad he didn't.

His parents died when he was 10 and he spent a few years after that being mostly quiet and doing what work he could find. He'd learned early that a certain expression at a certain moment could get a villager to press a few coins into his hand out of sympathy, and he used this when he needed to without feeling too bad about it. By 14 he didn't need to anymore; the construction work in the nearest city paid actual money and he could fish on the off-season. The city was about 14 hours on foot from the village — a large place, the fourth biggest in rice country, built on two rivers, full of people and construction sites and, probably now, a very large number of Level-whatever zombies.

He turns this over while he walks. The village has almost nothing left in it. The fish in the lake are growing. The house has a broken lock and no boat. Staying is just waiting for something to be stronger than him on a day when he's tired. The city is a long walk away, but it has buildings to shelter in, more supplies, and significantly more EXP walking around in it. The math on this is not complicated.

He shifts direction on the road toward the city and keeps going.

He rounds a bend in the road about twenty minutes later and finds a car crash: two vehicles across the road, the front ends crumpled together, one of them rolled onto its side. Scattered around the wreck are the remains of several people who didn't survive the initial impact. Under the side-rolled vehicle, a zombie is pinned from the waist down, upper body flat on the road, one arm reaching toward him as he approaches.

It's trying very hard for something without the ability to go anywhere. Lucas looks at it. "Nice, free experience," he remarks, and crouches a few feet away to think about how to approach this.

The spear is practical but he doesn't need it here. He's been curious about the dagger since it appeared. He takes it out of his pocket and crouches closer to the pinned zombie, coming around to the side of its head. The zombie tracks him with its eyes, teeth working. He drives the dagger into the eye socket and pushes the handle forward steadily. The eye gives way with a Squelch! and the blade finds the back of the skull. The zombie goes still.

Lucas looks at his hand. He straightens up. "I think I might be addicted to eye-popping now," he says, to no one in particular, in a tone of mild professional concern.

---

[Level 1 Zombie Killed, Awarding 50 Exp!]

[Received 5 Coins!]

[Leveling Exp Reach: 310/300!]

[Leveling Up: Level 5!]

[Receiving 2 Stats Points and 1 Skills Point!]

---

He looks at the dagger. Five coins, from a kill with the dagger, when none of his other kills have produced any. He could be wrong — it might just be that coins come from certain zombie levels, or certain locations, or any number of conditions he hasn't identified yet. He needs more data, which means he needs more zombies, which is not something he expected to be thinking about when he left the house this morning.

"This is getting more and more complicated," he mutters, wiping the dagger on the road surface and pocketing it. He stands up, looks at the city road stretching ahead, and starts walking again.

He allocates the new points while he walks. 2 stat points, and this time the system registers on the second attempt instead of the third, which he considers an improvement. He puts both into Vit. Str is above average now and Agi has closed some of the gap; what's been bothering him since the fish section is how long it takes to absorb a hit before his body catches up to what's happening. Vit seems like the right answer to that, even if he can't test it immediately.

"System, 2 points to vitality," he says.

---

[Adding 2 Stats Points to Vitality.]

---

Then the skill point. He's been putting this off because the system hasn't given him any information to work with and he'd rather know what he's doing before he does it. But the point has been sitting there since the market, unused, and he's walking a long empty road with nothing better to occupy the time.

"System, show skills," he tries. Nothing. "System, skill list." Nothing. "System, available skills." Still nothing. He tries four more variations over the next hundred meters, none of which produce a response. He tries specifying: "System, allocate skill point." This one gets a reply.

---

[No Skills Available for Allocation.]

[Skills are acquired through repeated action. Continue performing actions to develop skills.]

---

Lucas reads it twice. He reads it a third time, more slowly. "So the system could have told me that at any point in the last two days," he says. The system does not appear to consider this an actionable input. He closes it and keeps walking.

Repeated action. He runs a quick count in his head: spear kills, bat kills, one dagger kill, one shampoo-bottle throw he'd rather not think too hard about. Apparently none of that has crossed whatever threshold the system is measuring. He'd complain about this but the road is empty and complaining twice at the same problem in one afternoon seems excessive. He files it under things that will resolve themselves and focuses on the ground ahead.

The road runs straight for a long stretch through flat land on either side, dry grass pressed flat against the shoulder in the wind. He passes two more cars that have run off the road, one of them nose-first into the ditch, the other stalled sideways across a lane with all its doors open. Both are empty. He checks each one as he passes, quickly, spear ready, and moves on when nothing moves back.

Afternoon settles in and the air gets heavier. He takes a water bottle out of the pouch and drinks while he walks, which still produces a mild sense of wrongness. Something about reaching into a flat pocket and pulling out a full 1.5-litre bottle. He tries not to think about it and mostly succeeds.

He hears the mutated cat before he sees it.

A low sound from the top of a parked delivery truck parked on the road shoulder, more continuous than a normal cat's call and with a rougher edge underneath it. Lucas slows without stopping and looks up at the truck roof. Two eyes catch the light back at him from the edge. The cat is sitting there watching him, and it's about twice the size it should be.

He stops walking. The cat's tail flicks once. 'Mutated cat, unknown level.' He does not know how fast a mutated cat moves and he's moderately sure he doesn't want to find out on a flat open road with no cover. The regular cats he's wanted to own his whole life were already faster than him. He looks at the truck, at the cat, and then at the road stretching clear and open past the truck.

He goes around the truck on the far side, slowly, keeping the vehicle between himself and the roof, spear out at his side. The cat makes the sound again from above, tracking him as he moves. He gets past the rear bumper and waits. Nothing drops down. He picks his pace back up and doesn't look back until he's a reasonable distance down the road.

The cat is still on the truck. It watches him go.

"I like you better from a distance," he calls back, and the cat says nothing.

***

The light is going orange by the time he finds the roadside shelter: a covered bus stop with a concrete bench on each side, set back slightly from the road under a corrugated iron roof. It's not much, but it faces the road in both directions and has solid walls on three sides. He's been walking for around six hours. The city is still far ahead, a faint thickening on the horizon where the roads begin to multiply.

He sits on the bench and opens the pouch and eats: a tin of canned meat, most of a bag of sunflower seeds, and the orange juice, which has been warm since he took it out of the freezer but is still fine. He checks the system while he eats.

---

Name: Lucas Tharla

Age: 16 years old

Race: Human

Gender: Male

Class: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Subclass: None

Level: 5 (10/400)

Stats:

Agi: 14 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Str: 24 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Int: 25 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Vit: 27 (Average Adult Human: 20)

Unallocated Stats Point: 0

Skills:

Unallocated Skills Point: 1

Coins: 5

Achievements Completed: 1 ᐃ

Items: 2 ᐃ

---

Vit at 27, above average. Agi still the low point, which he knows. One skill point still sitting there, unused, because the system says actions develop skills and he hasn't done enough of any one action to qualify for whatever threshold it's measuring. He'll find out when he finds out.

The road is quiet in both directions. He finishes eating, puts the empty tin in the pouch, and lies down on the bench with the spear across his chest and the bat handle propped against the wall within reach. The shelter's iron roof ticks in the cooling air. He listens to the road for a few minutes and hears nothing on it.

He closes his eyes. The city is still hours away and he intends to be moving again before sunrise. Whatever is in it — high-level zombies, other survivors, something worse — it'll still be there in the morning.
